sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a male, presented with neck pain after a recent car crash in February of this year.
He reports experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain, rated at a 12 out of 10, which has prevented him from working.
He has a history of multiple car crashes, including one in which he underwent surgery.
He reports stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and spasms throughout his body.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of car crashes, including a recent one in February of this year.
He underwent surgery after a previous crash and has been in therapy.
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness, and spasms.
Current medications: He was previously prescribed fentanyl, but has not taken it for several weeks.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and muscle.
He has moderate range of movement in his neck, but experiences pain when bending forward and backwards.
There is no bruising, swelling, or laceration observed.
Test Results: The patient had an x-ray before coming in, which did not show a fracture.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which can be explained by the recent car crash.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours for muscle relaxation and pain relief.
I also instructed the patient to apply ice to the affected area and perform exercises to maintain range of motion.
We will refer the patient to physical therapy and order an MRI to further evaluate the condition.
We will also explore local injections and refer the patient to pain management if necessary.
The patient will be given a note to excuse him from work until the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen after the MRI results are available to discuss further treatment options.
